headline_verdict: SWDI web service does NOT serve the Storm Events Database. The Storm Events Database is ONLY available as per-YEAR bulk gzip CSV files (no point/date query). Recommend documented TODO, not a v1 point+date source. SWDI is a separate, usable point+date source for NEXRAD radar SIGNATURES (hail/TVS/MDA), which is a different product than confirmed storm reports.

A_storm_events_bulk_csv.CRITICAL_c_suffix: The c{YYYYMMDD} creation-date suffix is NOT predictable from the year. Observed: d2024->c20260421, d2025->c20260526, d2026->c20260519, d2021/22/23->c20260323. You MUST GET the directory listing and regex the real filename; do not construct it.
A_storm_events_bulk_csv.modern_year_size_gz: ~12.7 MB gzipped (d2024). Uncompressed ~80-100 MB. d1950 only 10 KB gz.
A_storm_events_bulk_csv.update_cadence: yearly file rewritten monthly; c-date changes signal new/corrected data
A_storm_events_bulk_csv.geo_cols: BEGIN_LAT (idx44), BEGIN_LON (idx45), END_LAT (idx46), END_LON (idx47) -- decimal degrees WGS84-ish, quoted strings
A_storm_events_bulk_csv.key_value_cols: EVENT_TYPE (e.g. 'Tornado','Hail','Thunderstorm Wind'), MAGNITUDE (numeric str; hail size inches / wind kt), MAGNITUDE_TYPE (EG/MG/MS/ES for wind), TOR_F_SCALE (F0..F5/EF0..EF5)
A_storm_events_bulk_csv.sentinels: Empty fields are empty quoted ''/blank, not -999. DAMAGE_* are strings like '250K','25K','0','1.50M'. Many geo cols blank for county/zone (CZ_TYPE='Z') events -- only point-type ('C') events reliably have lat/lon.
A_storm_events_bulk_csv.format_gotchas: All values quoted strings; BEGIN_DATE_TIME like '28-APR-50 14:45:00' (2-digit year, CST/local tz given by CZ_TIMEZONE, NOT UTC). Lat/lon present only for some rows. No header of UTC -- times are LOCAL per CZ_TIMEZONE.
A_storm_events_bulk_csv.feasibility_for_point_date: NOT feasible as a live per-request source: a point+date query requires downloading and scanning the entire ~12.7MB-gz / ~80MB year file, then filtering by date + bounding box on BEGIN_LAT/BEGIN_LON. Recommend: documented TODO / opt-in historical tool that downloads+caches a year file, NOT a v1 hot-path source.

B_swdi_web_service.does_it_serve_storm_events: NO. SWDI has no Storm Events Database product.

real_datasets_from_validator_and_xml.nx3structure: Filtered Storm Cells (max refl >=45 dBZ), Level-III
real_datasets_from_validator_and_xml.nx3hail: Filtered Hail Signatures (MaxSize>0 & Prob=100%), Level-III hail product
real_datasets_from_validator_and_xml.nx3meso: Mesocyclone Signatures (Level-III Meso) -- RETURNED 0 ROWS even on big 2011-05-24 OK outbreak bbox; appears empty/superseded
real_datasets_from_validator_and_xml.nx3mda: Digital Mesocyclone Detection Algorithm (Level-III MDA) -- RICH, this is the working meso product
real_datasets_from_validator_and_xml.nldn: Vaisala NLDN lightning -- HTTP 400 empty body, NOAA-VPN-only, NOT publicly accessible

B_swdi_web_service.doc_says_warn_but_validator_rejects: Usage docs mention a 'warn' (warnings polygon) dataset, but live validator returns HTTP 500 'ERROR VALIDATING product' and 'warn' is NOT in the acceptable list. DO NOT use 'warn' via SWDI. (Use NWS api.weather.gov for warnings instead.)
B_swdi_web_service.period_of_record: All nx3* datasets: 19920507 .. 20260611 (i.e. begins 1992-05-07, ends ~2 days ago). nldn: 19860101 .. 20260611. ~2 day latency.
B_swdi_web_service.point_date_query: YES -- works cleanly. Two spatial filters: tile=lon,lat (rounds to nearest 0.1deg, a 0.1x0.1 deg cell) OR bbox=minLon,minLat,maxLon,maxLat (order strictly enforced since 2016). Date range startDate inclusive, endDate EXCLUSIVE, GMT, max 1 year span. yyyymmdd or yyyymmddhhmi.
B_swdi_web_service.nx3hail_schema: ZTIME,WSR_ID,CELL_ID,PROB,SEVPROB,MAXSIZE,LAT,LON  (MAXSIZE inches; PROB/SEVPROB percent; ZTIME ISO Z/UTC; LAT/LON dec deg WGS84)
B_swdi_web_service.nx3tvs_schema: ZTIME,WSR_ID,CELL_ID,CELL_TYPE,RANGE,AZIMUTH,MAX_SHEAR,MXDV,LAT,LON (MXDV = max delta velocity kt; RANGE nm; AZIMUTH deg; some rows have blank LON)
B_swdi_web_service.sentinels: nx3mda uses -999 for missing MOTION_DEG/MOTION_KTS; TVS column is 'Y'/'N'. SWDI CSV always appends a trailing 'summary\ncount,N\ntotalTimeInSeconds,X' block -- MUST strip it before CSV parse. geojson is CRS84 (lon,lat order).
B_swdi_web_service.stat_and_filter: stat=count | stat=tilesum:lon,lat | stat=countGroupBy:COL ; filterBy=COL:VAL ; filterBy[]= repeatable (OR within col, AND across cols).
B_swdi_web_service.feasibility_for_point_date: YES feasible and cheap. But this is RADAR-DERIVED SIGNATURES, not confirmed ground-truth reports. Useful as a different layer (algorithmic hail/tornado/meso detections), NOT a substitute for Storm Events confirmed reports or NWS LSRs.
